name: Pixi
description: Install Pixi, the open-source, cross-platform package and project manager for conda and PyPI packages. Pixi provides fast, reproducible environments for Python, C++, R, and any language distributed via conda channels.

version.description:
Version of Pixi to install (e.g. '0.67.0').
Accepts 'latest' (resolved via the GitHub Releases API) or any 'X.Y.Z', 'X.Y', 'vX.Y.Z', or 'vX.Y' version string.
If the already-installed Pixi version matches the resolved version,
the install is skipped silently regardless of the 'if_exists' setting.

prefix.description:
Installation prefix for pixi. The 'pixi' binary is placed at '$prefix/bin/pixi'.
'auto' (default) — resolves to /usr/local when running as root, or $HOME/.pixi when running as non-root.
An explicit path — used as-is; the script exits with an error if the path is not writable and cannot be created.
Set to '' to use the upstream default ($HOME/.pixi) regardless of the current user.

if_exists.description:
What to do when a pixi binary already exists at $prefix/bin.
'skip'      -- log a warning and continue to post-install steps (default).
'fail'      -- print an error and exit non-zero.
'reinstall' -- remove the existing binary, then install fresh.
'update'    -- run 'pixi self-update --version <resolved>' to update in-place.
               Only works for installer-managed pixi (not Homebrew/conda/mamba).
Version-match: if the installed version already equals the resolved target
version, the install is always skipped silently regardless of this setting.

installer_dir.description:
Directory to download the pixi .tar.gz archive and its .tar.gz.sha256 sidecar to.
Both files are removed after installation unless keep_installer is true.

arch.description:
Override the detected CPU architecture when selecting a release asset.
Accepted values: 'x86_64', 'aarch64', 'riscv64'.
When empty (default), the architecture is detected automatically from uname -m.
Useful for cross-arch image builds or forced emulation only.

home_dir.description:
Set PIXI_HOME -- the directory where pixi stores global environments (pixi global install) and configuration.
When empty (default), the upstream default ($HOME/.pixi) is used.
PIXI_HOME is a runtime variable read by pixi on every invocation; set export_pixi_home='auto' (the default)
to have the installer write this to shell startup files so it is active in all future sessions.

download_url.description:
Override the binary download URL for the pixi .tar.gz archive.
When set, the standard GitHub Releases URL is not used and checksum
verification is skipped with a warning.
Useful for air-gapped environments, internal mirrors, or custom builds.

netrc.description:
Path to a .netrc file for authenticating the download request.
Passed to curl via --netrc-file or to wget via --netrc-file.
Use when downloading from a private mirror that requires credentials.
See https://pixi.prefix.dev/latest/installation/#using-netrc-for-authentication

export_path.description:
Controls which shell startup files receive the PATH export for $prefix/bin.
'auto'      -- writes to system-wide files (root) or user-scoped files (non-root).
             No-op when prefix resolves to /usr/local (already on PATH everywhere).
'' (empty)  -- skips all PATH writes.
Newline-separated list of absolute file paths -- writes only to those files.

export_pixi_home.description:
Controls which shell startup files receive 'export PIXI_HOME=<home_dir>'.
Only active when home_dir is non-empty; a no-op otherwise.
'auto'      -- writes to system-wide files (root) or user-scoped files (non-root).
'' (empty)  -- skips all PIXI_HOME writes.
Newline-separated list of absolute file paths -- writes only to those files.
PIXI_HOME is a runtime variable read on every pixi invocation; if it is not set
in the environment, pixi will not find globally installed tools or config written
to a custom home_dir.

symlink.description:
Create a symlink from the canonical bin directory to $prefix/bin/pixi when prefix resolves to a non-default path.
The symlink location is determined by whether $prefix falls under any user's home directory (as listed in /etc/passwd):
  System-wide (prefix is not under any user home): creates /usr/local/bin/pixi -> $prefix/bin/pixi.
  User-scoped  (prefix is under a user home):        creates $HOME/.pixi/bin/pixi -> $prefix/bin/pixi.
Ensures the containerEnv PATH entries always resolve to the installed binary regardless of the chosen prefix.
No-op when $prefix/bin/pixi is already the chosen target path.

shell_completions.description:
Space-separated list of shell names to write pixi completion eval blocks for.
For each listed shell, writes an idempotent 'eval "$(pixi completion --shell <shell>)"' block
to the appropriate system-wide (root) or user-scoped (non-root) shell config file.
Supported shells: 'bash', 'zsh', 'fish', 'nushell', 'elvish'.
Set to '' (the default) to skip all completion writes.
Examples: 'bash', 'zsh', 'bash zsh'.

keep_installer.description:
Keep the downloaded .tar.gz archive and .tar.gz.sha256 sidecar file after installation.
By default both files are removed from installer_dir on exit.

logfile.description: Log all output (stdout + stderr) to this file in addition to the console.
